# linkserver233

> Self-hosted, agent-first link server: long links, short links, custom path
> mapping, expiration, one-time/click limits, password protection, and a clean
> JSON API. Agents should call the JSON API instead of scraping HTML.

Install: https://github.com/neko233-com/linkserver233#installation

## Primary docs

- [README](https://github.com/neko233-com/linkserver233/blob/main/README.md): overview, install, full API reference
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/neko233-com/linkserver233/blob/main/AGENTS.md): when and how agents should use the API
- [Docs site](https://neko233-com.github.io/linkserver233/): hosted documentation

## Agent quick start

1. `GET /healthz` — confirm the server is up.
2. Send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on `/api/v1/*` when a token is configured.
3. `POST /api/v1/links` with `{"target_url": "..."}` to shorten; add `path`,
   `expires_in`, `max_clicks`, or `password` as needed.
4. Parse the JSON response — `short_url`, `status`, `expires_at`,
   `remaining_clicks` are always present.

## Common tasks

- Shorten: `POST /api/v1/links {"target_url":"https://..."}`
- Custom path: `POST /api/v1/links {"path":"docs/latest","target_url":"https://..."}`
- Expiring: add `{"expires_in":"24h"}` or `{"expires_at":"<RFC3339>"}`
- One-time: add `{"max_clicks":1}`
- Password: add `{"password":"secret"}`, visit with `?pw=secret` or `X-Link-Password`
- List/filter: `GET /api/v1/links?status=active&tag=docs&q=guide&limit=50&offset=0`
- Stats: `GET /api/v1/stats`
- Bulk import: `POST /api/v1/import`

## Install one-liners

- macOS/Linux: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neko233-com/linkserver233/main/scripts/install.sh | bash`
- Windows: `irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neko233-com/linkserver233/main/scripts/install.ps1 | iex`
